title: festuca ovina subsp. firmula
intro: Festuca ovina subsp. firmula, also known as sheep's fescue, is a subspecies of the Festuca ovina grass, native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Festuca ovina subsp. firmula include its ability to soothe coughs, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
